text.title: Freelance Marketplace Platform Tradeoffs Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Freelance Marketplace Platform Tradeoffs application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Match platform to rate band: Upwork ($20-100), Contra ($50-150), Arc ($75-200), Toptal ($75-250), Malt EU ($80-250), Braintrust ($75-200). Below or above platform's range = poor fit.
rule.rationale: Platforms cluster by buyer expectation; mismatch wastes ramp time on wrong-budget buyers.

rule.statement: Compare effective fee: Upwork 10% sliding, Toptal 0% (margin in buyer-side), Contra 0%, Malt 7-12%, Arc ~30%. Pick on net rate, not gross headline.
rule.rationale: Headline rate ≠ net rate; effective fee analysis prevents post-signup surprise.

rule.statement: Check exclusivity clauses: Toptal allows direct contract after platform intro, Braintrust DAO requires platform-only. Read T&Cs.
rule.rationale: Exclusivity locks revenue to one channel; founders frequently sign without reading.

rule.statement: Most platforms require 4-12 weeks of profile optimization + 5-10 proposals before first contract. Plan accordingly.
rule.rationale: Quitting after 2 weeks is the modal freelancer failure on platforms; commit or do not enter.

rule.statement: Pick ONE primary platform. Two = divided attention; three = no compounding. Secondary platforms are passive maintenance only.
rule.rationale: Algorithmic ranking on platforms favors active profiles; divided attention destroys both.
